action.reasoning: Observation: Previous reactive controller (depth-based) achieved non-zero safe_follow steps (26 in one episode) but episode ended early at step 546 (before 750), likely due to collision termination. avg safe_follow across 3 episodes ~11. No navmesh pathfinding works (empty waypoints), so global planning is unavailable. Analysis: Without navmesh, we must rely on reactive local obstacle avoidance. The current reactive controller collides and ends episodes prematurely, preventing safe_follow accumulation. We need better obstacle avoidance (wall-following) and collision-free navigation to keep the robot alive near the human. Decision: Implement a bug-like wall-following controller with state machine and depth-based obstacle avoidance. Test on 3 episodes; if avg safe_follow significantly increases, submit eval. Also add diagnostics to confirm navmesh unavailability and early termination reason.
